LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION FOR AN ORDER DISMISSING IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING MOTION. DISMISSAL IS NOT WARRANTED. DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES MOVE TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT BASED ON THE COURT ORDER, FILED ON MARCH 11, 2013, WHERE THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON, PC'S MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPPBACK COMPLAINT PER CCP 425.18. HOWEVER, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES WERE NOT THE MOVING PARTY ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE SLAPPBACK COMPLAINT. THE DISMISSAL OF THE COMPLAINT AGAINST DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES BASED ON THE COURT ORDER GRANTING MILANO AND LITTLER MENDELSON, PC'S MOTION IS NOT WARRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES ARGUE THE MERITS OF THEIR OWN ANTI-SLAPPBACK MOTION IN THE INSTANT MOTION TO DISMISS, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND GONCALVES DID NOT STATE THESE GROUNDS FOR RELIEF IN THEIR NOTICE OF MOTION. THE COURT CANNOT GRANT DIFFERENT RELIEF, OR RELIEF ON DIFFERENT GROUNDS, THAN STATED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. (LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CA4TH 1119, 1124, 132 CR2D 680, 684.) MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Ntc Of Anti Slappback Mtn For An Order Dismissing This Matter Inits Entirety, Or, In The Alt, Dismissing This Matter
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SEAN MORIARTY, ESQ., 650.991.5126, FOR DEFENDANTS CALTHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES; No other appearances.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES' and MARINELLA GONCALVES' Anti Slappback MOTION For An Order Dismissing This Matter In Its Entirety, Or, In The Alternative, Dismissing This Matter IS Denied. Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's SLAPPback complaint is untimely under CCP 425.18(d)(1)(A) and (B). Further Defendants' have not shown that extraordinary circumstances exist under CCP 426.18(d)(1)(C). The Complaint was filed January10, 2013. Moving parties filed a demurrer and motion to strike on February 22, 2013. A prior motion to dismiss was denied on August 20, 2013. This motion was filed November 12, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Dismissing This
Off Calendar Aug-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN L. DAHM OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 391-1113 X 12 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES and MARINELLA GONCALVES' Motion For An Order Dismissing IS Denied without prejudice of refiling motion. Dismissal is not warranted. Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves move to dismiss the Complaint based on the Court order, filed on March 11, 2013, where the Court granted Defendants Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint per CCP 425.18. However, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves were not the moving party on the motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint. The dismissal of the Complaint against Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves based on the Court Order granting Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion is not warranted. To the extent that Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves argue the merits of their own anti-SLAPPback motion in the instant motion to dismiss, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves did not state these grounds for relief in their Notice of Motion. The court cannot grant different relief, or relief on different grounds, than stated in the notice of motion. (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 CA4th 1119, 1124, 132 CR2d 680, 684.) Moving party to prepare form of order. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. The matter was discussed regarding the Order that was issued by the court on March 11, 2013. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES and MARINELLA GONCALVES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground for relief on a demurrer. (CCP 430.10. See Notice, p. 1:22 - 2:20.) (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN P. MORIARTY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 391-1113 X 15 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. The matter was discussed regarding the Order that was issued by the court on March 11, 2013. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES AND MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES OF THE COMPLAINT IS Denied. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground to strike a prayer for relief in the form of punitive damages. (CCP 436. See Notice, p. 2:22 - 3:16.) (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
